members.name: SetRefreshRate
members.brief: Sets the refresh-rate of the RenderTask.
members.detailed: The default is REFRESH_ALWAYS (1), meaning that the RenderTask will be processed every frame if the scene graph is changing. It may be desirable to process less frequently. For example, SetRefreshRate(3) will process once every 3 frames if the scene graph is changing. If the scene graph is not changing, then the render task will not be rendered, regardless of this value. The REFRESH_ONCE value means that the RenderTask will be processed once only, to take a snap-shot of the scene. Repeatedly calling SetRefreshRate(REFRESH_ONCE) will cause more snap-shots to be taken.

members.returns: The refresh-rate

members.detailed: Sets Render Pass key for this RenderTask. Shader code that matches this render pass is used for rendering. If no matching shader is found, the code with a render pass of 0 is used. In other cases, operation is not guaranteed.
notes: RenderPassTag of default RenderTask is 0u.

members.name: SetOrderIndex
members.detailed: Sets Order Index to define rendering order for this RenderTask. In the DALi, offscreen renderTasks are rendered earlier than onscreen renderTask. In each category of OffScreen RenderTask and OnScreen RenderTask, a RenderTask with a smaller orderIndex is rendered first. The RenderTasks in RenderTaskList is always sorted as acending order of the OrderIndex. The OrderIndex value is needed to be set between [-1000, 1000]. Default orderIndex is 0.
notes: The order among RenderTasks whose OrderIndex has not changed follows the order in which they were created.
Rendering order among RenderTasks those have same OrderIndex cannot be guaranteed after the OrderIndex is changed

params.name: orderIndex
params.description: the order index for this render task.

members.name: GetOrderIndex

members.returns: OrderIndex value for this render task.

compounds.name: Dali::RotationGesture
compounds.brief: A RotationGesture is emitted when the user moves two fingers that are opposite each other in a rotational/circular gesture.
compounds.detailed: This gesture can be in one of three states; when the rotation gesture is first detected, its state is set to GestureState::STARTED. After this, if there is change in the gesture, the state will be GestureState::CONTINUING. Finally, when the gesture ends, the state of the gesture changes to GestureState::FINISHED. A rotation gesture will continue to be sent to the actor under the center point of the rotation until the rotation ends.

The application programmer can use this gesture detector as follows: RotationGestureDetectordetector=RotationGestureDetector::New(); detector.Attach(myActor); detector.DetectedSignal().Connect(this,&MyApplication::OnRotation); Signals Signal Name Method rotationDetected DetectedSignal()

members.name: SetRotationCompletedAcknowledgement
members.brief: Send message to acknowledge for completing window rotation with current window orientation.
members.detailed: If this function is called, the message is sent to render thread, then mSurfaceRectChanged in scene-graph-scene is set with true. After that, render thread checks whether window rotation event is received, mSurfaceRectChanged and the neccessary flag are set. If they are all true, rotation done function is called to complete window rotation.

members.name: IsRotationCompletedAcknowledgementSet
members.brief: Query wheter is set to acknowledge for completing surface rotation.
members.detailed: If SetRotationCompletedAcknowledgement() is called and the related message is received to scene-graph-scene, then mSurfaceRectChanged in scene-graph-scene is set with true. When this function is called, the mSurfaceRectChanged in scene-graph-scene is return. Then, the flag will be reset.
notes: This function should be not called the application's main thread. Because this function should be called in windwow surface's postrender() function to complete window rotation manually.
members.returns: true it should be acknowledged.

members.name: KeepRendering
members.brief: Keep rendering for at least the given amount of time.
members.detailed: By default, Dali will stop rendering when no Actor positions are being set, and when no animations are running etc. This method is useful to force screen refreshes.

params.name: durationSeconds
params.description: Time to keep rendering, 0 means render at least one more frame

members.brief: This signal is emitted just after the event processing is finished.
members.returns: The signal to connect to

members.name: KeyEventSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when key event is received.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(constKeyEvent&event);
members.returns: The signal to connect to

members.name: KeyEventGeneratedSignal
members.brief: The user would connect to this signal to get a KeyEvent when KeyEvent is generated.
members.detailed: If the control already consumed key event, KeyEventProcessor do not need to Emit keyEvent. Therefore, KeyinputManager first checks whether KeyEvent is generated as KeyEventGeneratedSignal. After that keyEventProcessor must invoke KeyEvent only if KeyEventGeneratedSignal () is not consumed. A callback of the following type may be connected: boolYourCallbackName(constKeyEvent&event);
members.returns: The return is true if KeyEvent is consumed, otherwise false.

members.name: InterceptKeyEventSignal
members.brief: The user would connect to this signal to intercept a KeyEvent at window.
members.detailed: Intercepts KeyEvents in the window before dispatching KeyEvents to the control. If a KeyEvent is consumed, no KeyEvent is delivered to the control. A callback of the following type may be connected: boolYourCallbackName(constKeyEvent&event);
members.returns: The return is true if KeyEvent is consumed, otherwise false.

members.name: KeyEventMonitorSignal
members.brief: The user would connect to this signal to monitor a KeyEvent at window. This signal is emitted when KeyEvent is generated.
members.detailed: A Callback of the following type may be connected: boolYourCallbackName(constKeyEvent&event);
members.returns: The signal to connect to

members.name: TouchedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when the screen is touched and when the touch ends (i.e. the down & up touch events only).
members.detailed: If there are multiple touch points, then this will be emitted when the first touch occurs and then when the last finger is lifted. An interrupted event will also be emitted (if it occurs). A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(TouchEventevent);
notes: Motion events are not emitted.
members.returns: The touch signal to connect to

members.name: WheelEventSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when wheel event is received.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(constWheelEvent&event);
members.returns: The signal to connect to

members.name: WheelEventGeneratedSignal
members.brief: When a custom wheel event occurs, it need to process the focused actor first.
members.detailed: Therefore, KeyboardFocusManager first checks whether WheelEvent is generated as WheelEventGeneratedSignal. After that wheelEventProcessor must invoke WheelEvent only if wheelEventGeneratedSignal () is not consumed. This is only valid for custom wheel events. A callback of the following type may be connected: boolYourCallbackName(constWheelEvent&event);
members.returns: The return is true if WheelEvent is consumed, otherwise false.

members.name: Semaphore
members.brief: class constructor

params.name: desired
params.description: the desired initial value of the semaphore

members.name: Release
members.brief: Atomically increments the internal counter by the value of update.
members.detailed: Any thread waiting for the counter to be greater than 0 will subsequently be unlocked.

members.name: Acquire
members.brief: Atomically decrements the internal counter by one if it is greater than zero; otherwise blocks until it is greater than zero and can successfully decrement the internal counter.

members.name: TryAcquire
members.brief: Tries to atomically decrement the internal counter by one if it is greater than zero; no blocking occurs regardless.
members.returns: true if it decremented the counter, otherwise false.

members.name: TryAcquireFor
members.brief: Tries to atomically decrement the internal counter by one if it is greater than zero; otherwise blocks until it is greater than zero can successfully decrement the internal counter, or the relTime duration has been exceeded.
members.returns: true if it decremented the internal counter, otherwise false

params.name: relTime
params.description: the minimum duration the function must wait for to fail

members.name: TryAcquireUntil
members.brief: Tries to atomically decrement the internal counter by one if it is greater than zero; otherwise blocks until it is greater than zero can successfully decrement the internal counter, or the absTime duration point has been passed.
members.returns: true if it decremented the internal counter, otherwise false

params.name: absTime
params.description: the earliest time the function must wait until in order to fail

compounds.name: Dali::Signal
compounds.brief: Base Template class to provide signals.
compounds.detailed: To create a signal for this class, you first have to define a typedef within your handle's scope: classMyHandle:publicHandle { public: //Typedefs typedefSignal<void()>VoidSignalType; typedefSignal<bool()>BoolSignalType; typedefSignal<void(float)>ParamSignalType; ... public: //Signals VoidSignalType&VoidSignal(); BoolSignalType&BoolSignal(); ParamSignalType&ParamSignal(); ... }; The methods are required in the handle class so that the application writer can retrieve the Signal in order to connect/disconnect. In the implementation class, the members should be defined and the methods should be provided to retrieve the signal itself. These will be called by the equivalent methods in the MyHandle class. classMyObject:publicObject { ... public: MyHandle::VoidSignalType&VoidSignal() { returnmVoidSignal; } MyHandle::BoolSignalType&BoolSignal() { returnmBoolSignal; } MyHandle::ParamSignalType&ParamSignal() { returnmParamSignal; } private: //Signals MyHandle::VoidSignalTypemVoidSignal; MyHandle::BoolSignalTypemBoolSignal; MyHandle::ParamSignalTypemParamSignal; ... };

compounds.name: Dali::SignalConnection
compounds.brief: SignalConnection is the connection information held by the signal.
compounds.detailed: A signal can have zero to many connections, depending on how many slots are connected to this signal. A connection contains: Callback (slot) SignalObserver - interface provided by a slot owning object. It takes ownership of the callback, and will delete it when the connection is destroyed.

members.name: SignalConnection
members.brief: Constructor.

params.name: callback
params.description: The callback which should be a C function

members.name: SignalConnection
members.brief: Constructor.

params.name: callback
params.description: Ownership of this callback object is taken

members.name: ~SignalConnection
members.brief: Non-virtual destructor, not intended as a base class.

members.name: Disconnect
members.brief: Disconnects the signal from the slot.

params.name: slotObserver
params.description: The signal disconnecting from the slot

members.name: GetCallback
members.brief: Retrieves the callback.
members.returns: A pointer to the callback

members.name: SignalConnection
members.brief: Deleted copy constructor.

members.name: operator=
members.brief: Deleted copy assignment operator.

members.name: SignalConnection
members.brief: Move constructor.
members.detailed: A move constructor enables the resources owned by an rvalue object to be moved into an lvalue without copying.

params.name: connection
params.description: The property value to move from

members.name: operator=
members.brief: Move assignment operator.
members.returns: a reference to this

compounds.name: Dali::SignalConnectionBlock
compounds.brief: A block of SignalConnectionNode slots in a chained-block pool.
compounds.detailed: Blocks are allocated as contiguous memory: the block header followed by an array of SignalConnection slots. Blocks are chained in allocation order and never move once allocated, so pointers to slots are stable for the lifetime of the pool.

members.name: mNext
members.brief: Next block in chain.

members.name: mCapacity
members.brief: Number of slots in this block.

members.name: mHighWaterMark
members.brief: Number of slots ever used (iteration bound)

members.name: Slots
members.brief: Access the slot array that follows this header in memory.
members.returns: Pointer to the first SignalConnectionNode slot

members.name: Slots
members.brief: Access the slot array (const).
members.returns: Const pointer to the first SignalConnectionNode slot

compounds.name: Dali::SignalConnectionNode
compounds.brief: Wraps a SignalConnection with emission-order linked list indices.
compounds.detailed: BaseSignal maintains an intrusive doubly-linked list of these nodes to guarantee that callbacks fire in connection order. The pool allocates nodes; the linked list determines iteration order during Emit(). Uses uint32_t indices instead of pointers to reduce memory footprint:

members.name: INVALID_INDEX
members.brief: Sentinel for null/unlinked nodes.

members.name: connection
members.brief: The signal connection (owned)

members.name: mEmitNext
members.brief: Index of next node in emission-order list.

members.name: mEmitPrev
members.brief: Index of previous node in emission-order list.

compounds.name: Dali::SignalConnectionPool
compounds.brief: A lightweight chained-block memory pool for SignalConnection objects.
compounds.detailed: Provides O(1) allocation and deallocation of SignalConnection slots using an intrusive free list threaded through freed slot memory. Blocks double in capacity (2, 4, 8, 16, ...) following the FixedSizeMemoryPool pattern. Existing blocks never move, so slot pointers are stable for the pool's lifetime.

members.name: SignalConnectionPool
members.brief: Constructor.

members.name: ~SignalConnectionPool
members.brief: Destructor. Frees all block memory.
members.detailed: The caller (BaseSignal) must disconnect and free all live connections before destruction. The pool destructor only frees raw block memory.

members.name: SignalConnectionPool
members.brief: Deleted copy constructor

members.name: SignalConnectionPool
members.brief: Deleted move constructor

members.name: operator=
members.brief: Deleted copy assignment

members.name: operator=
members.brief: Deleted move assignment

members.name: Allocate
members.brief: Allocate a node and construct a SignalConnection with a callback.
members.detailed: Recycles from the free list if available, otherwise allocates from the current block (growing the pool if necessary).
members.returns: Pointer to the constructed node (stable for pool lifetime)

members.name: Allocate
members.brief: Allocate a node and construct a SignalConnection with tracker and callback.
members.returns: Pointer to the constructed node (stable for pool lifetime)

members.name: Free
members.brief: Free a node. Destructs the SignalConnectionNode and adds the slot to the free list.

params.name: node
params.description: Pointer to the node to free

members.name: GetFirstBlock
members.brief: Get the first block in the chain (for iteration).
members.returns: Pointer to the first block, or nullptr if empty

members.name: GetBlockCount
members.brief: Get the number of blocks allocated.
members.returns: The block count

members.name: IndexToNode
members.brief: Convert a global node index to a node pointer.
members.returns: Pointer to the node, or nullptr if index is invalid

members.name: NodeToIndex
members.brief: Convert a node pointer to its global index.
members.returns: The node's index, or 0xFFFF if not found

compounds.name: Dali::SignalConnectorType
compounds.brief: Registers a signal connector function to a registered type.

members.name: SignalConnectorType
members.brief: Constructor registers the type creation function.

compounds.name: Dali::SignalDelegate
compounds.brief: The SignalDelegate object allows direct connection to a signal that has been pre-configured internally.
compounds.detailed: EG: The SignalDelegate can be created internally and exposed to the application-developer. They can then call the connect function to transparently bind to their callback.

members.name: SignalDelegate
members.brief: Create and set up a signal delegate.

params.name: connectActor
params.description: The actor whose signal should be connected to.

params.name: signalName
params.description: The name of the signal within the actor to connect to.

members.name: ~SignalDelegate
members.brief: Destructor.

members.name: Connect
members.brief: Connect to a FunctorDelegate as received from a type-registry signal connection call.
members.detailed: This is required to allow connection to an actor's signal. Typically this is done in a generic way (IE. via a string of the signal name) using the ConnectSignal function. This function requires a functor.
members.returns: True of the connection was made, false otherwise.

params.name: connectionTracker
params.description: Passed in to the ConnectSignal function of the actor.

params.name: functorDelegate
params.description: A functor delegate object that must be executed when the signal is emitted.

members.name: Connect
members.brief: Connect to a non-static member function.
members.detailed: The object that owns the member function must also inherit from ConnectionTracker.
members.returns: True of the connection was made, false otherwise.

params.name: object
params.description: Object instance that houses the supplied member-function.

params.name: memberFunction
params.description: The member-function to call when the signal is emitted.

members.name: IsConnected
members.brief: Checks if this delegate has been connected yet, so it can be determined if it can be used or a new delegate must be created to set up a new connection (to the same signal).
members.returns: True if this SignalDelegate has been connected to it's signal.

members.name: Empty
members.brief: Queries whether there are any connected slots.
members.returns: True if there are any slots connected to the signal

members.name: GetConnectionCount
members.brief: Queries the number of slots.
members.returns: The number of slots connected to this signal

compounds.name: Dali::SlotConnection
compounds.brief: Slot connection is the connection information held by a connection tracker.
compounds.detailed: A slot can have zero to many connection objects, depending on how many signals it is connected to. A connection contains: Callback (slot) SlotObserver -interface provided by the signal It holds a pointer to the callback, but does not own it.

members.brief: Constructor.

compounds.name: Dali::SlotDelegate
compounds.brief: SlotDelegates can be used to connect member functions to signals, without inheriting from SlotDelegateInterface.
compounds.detailed: Note that the object providing the member function is expected to own the SlotDelegate; therefore when the object is destroyed, the SlotDelegate destructor will automatically disconnect. classExample//ThisdoesnotinheritfromSlotDelegateInterface! { public: Example() :mSlotDelegate(this) { } ~Example() { //mSlotDelegatedisconnectsautomaticallyhere } voidAnimate() { Animationanimation=Animation::New(1.0f); animation.FinishedSignal().Connect(mSlotDelegate,&Example::OnAnimationFinished); animation.Play();//fire&forget } voidOnAnimationFinished(Animation&animation) { std::cout<<"AnimationFinished!"<<std::endl; } private: SlotDelegate<Example>mSlotDelegate; };

compounds.name: Dali::SpringData
compounds.brief: Structure for custom spring parameters. This allows defining a custom spring-based easing curve using physics parameters.

members.name: SpringData
members.brief: Creates a spring data.
members.signature: SpringData(float stiffness, float damping, float mass)

params.name: stiffness
params.description: Spring stiffness(Hooke's constant). Higher values make the spring snap back faster. Minimum value is 0.1.

params.name: damping
params.description: Damping coefficient. Controls oscillation and settling. Minimum value is 0.1.

params.name: mass
params.description: Mass of the object. Affects inertia and the duration of the motion. Minimum value is 0.1.

members.name: SpringData
members.brief: Default constructor.

members.name: ~SpringData
members.brief: Destructor.

members.name: SpringData
members.brief: Copy constructor. Creates a new SpringData object by copying the contents of another.

params.name: rhs
params.description: The SpringData object to copy from.

members.name: SpringData
members.brief: Move constructor. Creates a new SpringData object by moving the contents of another.

params.name: rhs
params.description: The SpringData object to move from.

members.name: operator=
members.brief: Copy assignment operator. Copies the contents of another SpringData object to this one.
members.returns: A reference to this object.

params.name: rhs
params.description: The SpringData object to copy from.

members.name: operator=
members.brief: Move assignment operator. Moves the contents of another SpringData object to this one.
members.returns: A reference to this object.

params.name: rhs
params.description: The SpringData object to move from.

members.name: SetStiffness
members.brief: Sets the stiffness.

params.name: stiffness
params.description: Spring stiffness(Hooke's constant). Higher values make the spring snap back faster. Minimum value is 0.1.

members.name: GetStiffness
members.brief: Returns the stiffness.
members.returns: Spring stiffness(Hooke's constant). Higher values make the spring snap back faster. Minimum value is 0.1.

members.name: SetDamping
members.brief: Sets the damping.

params.name: damping
params.description: Damping coefficient. Controls oscillation and settling. Minimum value is 0.1.

members.name: GetDamping
members.brief: Returns the damping.
members.returns: Damping coefficient. Controls oscillation and settling. Minimum value is 0.1.

members.name: SetMass
members.brief: Sets the mass.

params.name: mass
params.description: Mass of the object. Affects inertia and the duration of the motion. Minimum value is 0.1.

members.name: GetMass
members.brief: Returns the mass.
members.returns: Mass of the object. Affects inertia and the duration of the motion. Minimum value is 0.1.

members.name: GetDuration
members.brief: Returns the time in seconds it takes for a Spring Animation to converge based on the SpringData. The maximum value for the returned duration is 100 seconds. Since this value is calculated in an incremental manner, it may take longer if used frequently.

compounds.name: Dali::Stage
compounds.brief: The Stage is a top-level object used for displaying a tree of Actors.
compounds.detailed: Stage is a top-level object that represents the entire screen. It is used for displaying a hierarchy of actors managed by the scene graph structure, which means an actor inherits a position relative to its parent, and can be moved in relation to this point. The stage instance is a singleton object (the only instance of its class during the lifetime of the program). You can get it using a static function. To display the contents of an actor, it must be added to a stage. The following example shows how to connect a new actor to the stage: Actoractor=Actor::New(); Stage::GetCurrent().Add(actor); The stage has a 2D size that matches the size of the application window. The default unit 1 is 1 pixel with the default camera. Multiple stage/window support is not currently provided. Signals Signal Name Method keyEvent KeyEventSignal() eventProcessingFinished EventProcessingFinishedSignal() touched TouchedSignal() wheelEvent WheelEventSignal() contextLost ContextLostSignal() contextRegained ContextRegainedSignal() sceneCreated SceneCreatedSignal()

compounds.name: Dali::String
compounds.detailed: A wrapper around the standard string object, to ensure future binary compatibility. The string data is stored in opaque inline storage, avoiding heap allocation for the wrapper itself. If the underlying std::string implementation changes size in a future toolchain, the implementation can fall back to heap allocation without changing this header. Some basic operators are provided for simple use cases.

compounds.name: Dali::Integration::TimeChecker::ThresholdFilter
compounds.brief: Print as release info if elapsed time greater or equal than threshold. (milliseconds) You can also set threshold by environment value.
compounds.detailed: threshold = -1 (==std::numeric_limits<uint32_t>::max()) means always not print. threshold = 0 mean always print. To check duration, call BeginTimeCheck() and EndTimeCheck() pairwise. The filter print duration at EndTimeCheck() by DALI_LOG_RELEASE_INFO. We can call it recursively. auto*filter=ThresholdFilter::New(20u,"specific_environment_value"); filter->BeginTimeCheck(); for(;;) { filter->BeginTimeCheck(); //Somecodes filter->EndTimeCheck("Phase1"); filter->BeginTimeCheck(); //Somecodes filter->EndTimeCheck("Phase2"); } filter->EndTimeCheck("Total");

members.name: New
members.brief: Create new filter object. The ownership of memory hold in global static list. It will be released when thread joined.
members.returns: New allocated filter with threshold and environment.

members.returns: True if threshold is valid value. False if threshold is -1 (==std::numeric_limits<uint32_t>::max())

members.name: EndTimeCheck

compounds.brief: Touch events are a collection of points at a specific moment in time.
compounds.detailed: When a multi-touch event occurs, each point represents the points that are currently being touched or the points where a touch has stopped. members.name: GetPressure
members.brief: Retrieves the touch pressure.
members.detailed: The pressure range starts at 0.0f. Normal pressure is defined as 1.0f. A value between 0.0f and 1.0f means light pressure has been applied. A value greater than 1.0f means more pressure than normal has been applied.
notes: If point is greater than GetPointCount() then this method will return 1.0f.
members.returns: The touch pressure

members.name: GetAngle

The internal object can change which may not be what an application writer expects. If data does need to be stored in the application, then only the required data should be saved (retrieved using the methods of this class).

compounds.name: Dali::Integration::TouchEventCombiner
compounds.detailed: Dali::Integration::TouchEventCombiner is a utility class, an instance of which, should be created upon initialisation. It accepts single Point(s) containing information about a touch area and creates a TouchEvent and/or HoverEvent combining the latest event's information with previous Point(s). The created TouchEvent and/or HoverEvent can then be sent to the Dali Core as indicated by the GetNextTouchEvent() method. The TouchEventCombiner ensures that the following rules are also adhered to: If two Down events are accidentally received, then the second one is ignored. Motion events are not processed if a Down event does not precede it. Motion event throttling is carried out to satisfy the minimum distance and time delta required. If an interrupted event is received, then any stored Point history is cleared.

compounds.name: Dali::TypeRegistry
compounds.brief: The TypeRegistry allows registration of type instance creation functions.
compounds.detailed: These can then be created later by name and down cast to the appropriate type. Usage: (Registering) In my-actor.cpp //Note:objectconstructioninnamespacescopeisdefinedinatranslationunitasbeing //inappearanceorder(C++standard3.6/2).SoTypeRegistrationisdeclaredfirstin //thecppfilebelow.Signal,actionandpropertydeclarationsfollowinanyorder. namespace { TypeRegistrationmyActorType(typeid(MyActor),typeid(Actor),CreateMyActor); SignalConnectorType(myActorType,"highlighted",ConnectSignalForMyActor); TypeAction(myActorType,"open",DoMyActorAction); TypeAction(myActorType,"close",DoMyActorAction); PropertyRegistration(myActorType,"status",PropertyRegistration::START_INDEX,Property::BOOLEAN,SetPropertyFunction,GetPropertyFunction); } Usage: (Creation) TypeRegistry::TypeInfotype=TypeRegistry::Get().GetTypeInfo("MyActor"); MyActora=MyActor::DownCast(type.CreateInstance()); if(a) { ... } CustomActor Actors that inherit from the CustomActor framework must ensure the implementation class has an identical name to the Actor class. This is to ensure the class can be found at runtime for signals and actions. Otherwise these will silently fail. As namespaces are discarded the convention is to use a namespace ie 'class MyActor {}; namespace Internal { class MyActor {}; }' Warning: this arrangement will silently fail 'class MyActor {}; class MyActorImpl {};' Naming Conventions Signal and action names follow properties and are by convention lower case hyphen separated ie 'next-page'. This maintains consistency with the scripted interface.

compounds.name: Dali::UniformBlock
compounds.detailed: Uniform block is a property owning object that can hold a number of properties mapping onto a uniform block in the shader. Mapping is done automatically through shader reflection, the structure doesn't need to be defined here. (Order of property registration is not relevant) Uniform Properties can be animated / constrained as normal. The underlying code requires the name of the uniform block to match; consequently, it's a construct only property. When the uniform block object is connected to a shader, it will be used to populate the uniforms for that shader, no matter what renderer actor that shader is connected to. Consequently, the client does not need to declare the block's properties on the shader, renderer or actor. Furthermore, any such declared properties will not override those in the Uniform Block.

compounds.name: Dali::UpdateProxy
compounds.brief: This class is used to access data of the actors from the update-thread.
compounds.detailed: The Actor API CANNOT be called directly from the update-thread. This class can be used as a proxy to that required Actor data. An actor's data can be accessed using the Actor's Unique ID. The unique ID should be passed to the callback using this class in a thread-safe manner (as you cannot call Actor::GetId from the update-thread).

members.name: NotifySyncPoint
members.detailed: Type to define a unique sync point.

members.name: GetPosition

members.returns: Whether the method call was successful or not.

params.name: position
params.description: Set to the Actor's current position, if Actor ID is valid

members.name: SetPosition
members.brief: Allows setting an Actor's local position from the Frame callback function for the current frame only.
notes: This will get reset to the internally calculated or previously baked value in the next frame, so will have to be set again.
members.returns: Whether the method call was successful or not.

members.name: BakePosition

notes: The value is saved so will cause undesired effects if this property is being animated.
members.returns: Whether the method call was successful or not.

members.name: Insert
members.brief: Inserts an element to the vector using move semantics.
members.detailed: Elements after at are moved one position to the right. The underlying storage may be reallocated to provide space. If this occurs, all pre-existing pointers into the vector will become invalid.

params.description: Iterator where to insert the element into the vector

members.detailed: Elements after at are moved the number of given elements positions to the right. The underlying storage may be reallocated to provide space. If this occurs, all pre-existing pointers into the vector will become invalid.

params.description: Iterator where to insert the elements into the vector

params.description: Iterator to the first element to be inserted

params.name: to
params.description: Iterator to the last element to be inserted

members.detailed: Reserving less than current Capacity is a no-op.

members.brief: Resizes the vector. Does not change capacity.

members.brief: Resizes the vector without initializing the data.
members.detailed: Can be used as a data container for reading whole file content.
notes: Only able for trivial types.

members.name: Resize
members.brief: Resizes the vector. Does not change capacity.

params.name: item
params.description: An item to insert to the new indices

members.name: Erase
members.brief: Erases an element.
members.detailed: Does not change capacity. Other elements get moved.
members.returns: Iterator pointing to next element

params.name: iterator
params.description: Iterator pointing to the item to remove

members.name: Erase
members.brief: Erases a range of elements.
members.detailed: Does not change capacity. Other elements get moved.
members.returns: Iterator pointing to the next element of the last one

params.name: first
params.description: Iterator to the first element to be erased

params.name: last
params.description: Iterator to the last element to be erased

members.name: Remove
members.brief: Removes an element.
members.detailed: Does not maintain order. Swaps the element with end and decreases size by one. This is much faster than Erase so use this in case order does not matter. Does not change capacity.

params.name: iterator
params.description: Iterator pointing to the item to remove

members.name: Swap
members.brief: Swaps the contents of two vectors.

members.name: Clear
members.brief: Clears the contents of the vector. Keeps its capacity.

members.name: Release
members.brief: Releases the memory that the vector holds.

members.name: ShrinkToFit
members.brief: Fit the capacity of vector as item counts.

members.name: ShrinkToFitIfNeeded
members.brief: Fit the capacity of vector as item counts only if low spec memory management enabled, and the count is much smaller than capacity. It will be used when we want to remove unused memory.

compounds.name: Dali::VectorBase

compounds.detailed: Memory layout is such that it has two SizeType values to hold the count and capacity of the vector. VectorBase::mData is adjusted so that it points to the beginning of the first real item so that iterating the items is quick.

compounds.name: Dali::VertexBuffer
compounds.brief: VertexBuffer is a handle to an object that contains a buffer of structured data.
compounds.detailed: VertexBuffers can be used to provide data to Geometry objects. Example: const float halfQuadSize = .5f; struct TexturedQuadVertex { Vector2 position; Vector2 textureCoordinates; }; TexturedQuadVertex texturedQuadVertexData[4] = { { Vector2(-halfQuadSize, -halfQuadSize), Vector2(0.f, 0.f) }, { Vector2( halfQuadSize, -halfQuadSize), Vector2(1.f, 0.f) }, { Vector2(-halfQuadSize, halfQuadSize), Vector2(0.f, 1.f) }, { Vector2( halfQuadSize, halfQuadSize), Vector2(1.f, 1.f) } }; Property::Map texturedQuadVertexFormat; texturedQuadVertexFormat["aPosition"] = Property::VECTOR2; texturedQuadVertexFormat["aTexCoord"] = Property::VECTOR2; VertexBuffer texturedQuadVertices = VertexBuffer::New( texturedQuadVertexFormat ); texturedQuadVertices.SetData( texturedQuadVertexData, 4 ); // Create indices uint32_t indexData[6] = { 0, 3, 1, 0, 2, 3 }; // Create the geometry object Geometry texturedQuadGeometry = Geometry::New(); texturedQuadGeometry.AddVertexBuffer( texturedQuadVertices ); texturedQuadGeometry.SetIndexBuffer( indexData, sizeof(indexData)/sizeof(indexData[0] );

members.name: SetData
members.brief: Updates the whole buffer information.
members.detailed: This function expects a pointer to an array of structures with the same format that was given in the construction, and the number of elements to be the same as the size of the buffer. If the initial structure was: { { "position", VECTOR3}, { "uv", VECTOR2 } } and a size of 10 elements, this function should be called with a pointer equivalent to: struct Vertex { Dali::Vector3 position; Dali::Vector2 uv; }; Vertex vertices[ 10 ] = { ... }; vertexBuffer.SetData( vertices );

members.name: SetDivisor
members.brief: Sets vertex divisor for all attributes.
members.detailed: If instancing isn't supported, the function has no effect. It's responsibility of developer to make sure the feature is supported. A divisor of 0 will turn off instanced drawing. Currently, a divisor > 1 will turn on instanced draw, but will have an actual rate of 1.

params.name: divisor
params.description: Sets vertex buffer divisor for an instanced draw

members.name: GetDivisor
members.brief: Get the divisor for the given attribute. A return value of 0 means that instancing is turned off.
members.returns: either 0 (not instanced), or > 0 (instanced)

compounds.name: Dali::VertexBufferUpdateCallback
compounds.detailed: The class defines a callback that VertexBuffer object may call to obtain new data. The callback runs before draw call is issued and it will run on update/render thread (developers must synchronize explicitly). Callback returns number of bytes written. This will limit next draw call to number of elements that have been written by the callback. Using callback invalidates current vertex buffer data. Unchanged data stays undefined.

compounds.name: Dali::WeakHandleBase
compounds.brief: Base class to store a weak pointer to an internal DALi object.

compounds.name: Dali::WheelEvent
compounds.brief: The wheel event class is used to store a wheel rolling, it facilitates processing of the wheel rolling and passing to other libraries like Toolkit.
compounds.detailed: There is a key modifier which relates to keys like alt, shift and control functions are supplied to check if they have been pressed when the wheel is being rolled. We support a mouse device and there may be another custom device that support the wheel event. The device type is specified as type. The mouse wheel event can be sent to the specific actor but the custom wheel event will be sent to the stage.

compounds.name: Dali
compounds.brief: The DALi namespace.
compounds.detailed: < for std::chrono::time_point < for uint32_t, size_t The class should implement Dali::ConnectionTrackerInterface, or inherit from Dali::ConnectionTracker. < Dali::Min , Dali::Max < for time(nullptr) < Dali::Swap < for std::move() < for srand() and rand() As the Implementation is bit complex because of Small Buffer Optimization (SBO) In the Property::Value class and Tagged Union implementation in the Impl class. Here is the brief description of the motivation and internal of this implementation. The main motivation is to keep the value semantic without doing any heap allocation. In the public class we keep a aligned buffer of 16byte instead of keeping the Pimpl pointer. we create the Impl object inplace in that buffer to avoid the memory allocation. we don't store the Impl* along with the SBO storage to save 8byte from the Property::Value object. Every time we need to access Impl object we access it through Read() and Write() which retrieves the already constructed Impl object from the SBO storage. with SBO , the move and assignment operator are tricky , so we use std::copy to move the object and update the moved from object to NONE to keep it in a safe state. In the Impl class, we keep a Uninitialized Union and manage lifecycle of objects by doing construct/destroy manually. We keep the Type information along with the Object to keep the size as 16byte (using common initial sequence(CIS) ). This enforces automatic disconnection when an object is destroyed, so you don't have to manually disconnect from signals. Alternatively, you can use a Dali::SlotDelegate if you don't want to inherit. E.g: classMyClass:publicConnectionTracker { voidCallback(Actoractor,constTouchEvent&event) { ... } voidInit() { Actoractor=Actor::New(); actor.TouchedSignal().Connect(this,&MyClass::Callback); } ~MyClass() { //ConnectionTrackerbaseclassautomaticallydisconnects } } The top level DALi namespace

members.name: CalculateScreenPosition
members.brief: Calculate the current position of the actor in screen coordinates using event-side properties.
members.detailed: This function calculates the screen coordinates of an actor by automatically detecting whether to use 2D or 3D calculation based on the actor's context and ancestor layers. For 2D calculation (when no 3D layers in ancestors): Uses 2D-specific optimizations Assumes orthographic projection and no camera rotation Z coordinates are treated as 0 for screen position calculation Faster calculation with 2D limitations For 3D calculation (when 3D layers exist in ancestors): Uses full 3D projection with view and projection matrices Supports perspective projection, camera rotation, and full 3D transforms Considers Z depth for proper screen coordinate projection More accurate but computationally expensive
members.returns: Returns the screen position of actor. Returns (0,0) if actor is not on scene.

members.name: CalculateScreenExtents
members.detailed: Calculates screen position and size using event-side properties. This function calculates the bounding box screen coordinates and size of an actor by automatically detecting whether to use 2D or 3D calculation based on the actor's context and ancestor layers. For 2D calculation (when no 3D layers in ancestors): Uses 2D-specific optimizations Assumes orthographic projection and no camera rotation Transforms 4 corner points in 2D space (Z=0) for bounding box calculation Faster calculation with 2D limitations For 3D calculation (when 3D layers exist in ancestors): Uses full 3D projection with view and projection matrices Supports perspective projection, camera rotation, and full 3D transforms Transforms all 8 corners of the 3D bounding box for accurate screen extents Accounts for perspective foreshortening and 3D rotation effects More accurate but computationally expensive
members.returns: Rect containing position of top-left corner on screen and size respectively. Returns (0,0,0,0) if actor is not on scene.

members.name: CalculateCurrentScreenExtents
members.detailed: Calculates screen position and size from current node values. This function calculates the bounding box screen coordinates and size of an actor using the current state from the update thread (node). It automatically detects whether to use 2D or 3D calculation based on the actor's context and ancestor layers. For 2D calculation (when no 3D layers in ancestors): Uses 2D-specific optimizations Assumes orthographic projection and no camera rotation Transforms 4 corner points in 2D space (Z=0) for bounding box calculation Uses current node state for the specified buffer index Faster calculation with 2D limitations For 3D calculation (when 3D layers exist in ancestors): Uses full 3D projection with view and projection matrices Supports perspective projection, camera rotation, and full 3D transforms Transforms all 8 corners of the 3D bounding box for accurate screen extents Accounts for perspective foreshortening and 3D rotation effects Uses current node state for the specified buffer index More accurate but computationally expensive
members.returns: Rect containing position of top-left corner on screen and size respectively. Returns (0,0,0,0) if actor is not on scene.

members.name: VisibilityChangedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when the visible property of this or a parent actor is changed.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Actoractor,boolvisible,VisibilityChange::Type&type); actor: The actor, or child of actor, whose visibility has changed. visible: If type is SELF, then this is true if this actor's VISIBILITY property is true. If Type is PARENT, this is true if a parent's VISIBILITY property has changed to true. type: Whether the actor's visible property has changed or a parent's.
notes: This signal is NOT emitted if the actor becomes transparent (or the reverse), it's ONLY linked with Actor::Property::VISIBLE.
For reference, an actor is only shown if it and it's parents (up to the root actor) are also visible, are not transparent, and this actor has a non-zero size.
members.returns: The signal to connect to

members.name: GetVisiblityChangedActor
members.brief: Get the actor who trigger the VisibilityChangedSignal or InheritedVisibilityChangedSignal signal.
members.detailed: For example, Let we assume some Actor tree looks like (root)A - B - C - D - E. If we change C's visibility as false + Change A's visibility inside of D's VisibilityChangedSignal callback, The result of this API will like this. (Some codes here) --> GetVisiblityChangedActor() is INVALID C.SetProperty(Actor::Property::VISIBLE, false) VisibilityChangedSignal(C, false, VisibilityChange::SELF) --> GetVisiblityChangedActor() is Actor C VisibilityChangedSignal(D, false, VisibilityChange::PARENT) --> GetVisiblityChangedActor() is Actor C A.SetProperty(Actor::Property::VISIBLE, false) VisibilityChangedSignal(A, false, VisibilityChange::SELF) --> GetVisiblityChangedActor() is Actor A VisibilityChangedSignal(B, false, VisibilityChange::PARENT) --> GetVisiblityChangedActor() is Actor A VisibilityChangedSignal(C, false, VisibilityChange::PARENT) --> GetVisiblityChangedActor() is Actor A VisibilityChangedSignal(D, false, VisibilityChange::PARENT) --> GetVisiblityChangedActor() is Actor A VisibilityChangedSignal(E, false, VisibilityChange::PARENT) --> GetVisiblityChangedActor() is Actor A InheritedVisibilityChangedSignal(A, false) --> GetVisiblityChangedActor() is Actor A InheritedVisibilityChangedSignal(B, false) --> GetVisiblityChangedActor() is Actor A (Some codes here) --> GetVisiblityChangedActor() is Actor C VisibilityChangedSignal(E, false, VisibilityChange::PARENT) --> GetVisiblityChangedActor() is Actor C InheritedVisibilityChangedSignal(C, false) --> GetVisiblityChangedActor() is Actor C InheritedVisibilityChangedSignal(D, false) --> GetVisiblityChangedActor() is Actor C InheritedVisibilityChangedSignal(E, false) --> GetVisiblityChangedActor() is Actor C (Some codes here) --> GetVisiblityChangedActor() is INVALID
notes: The return value is "INVALID" if this API called outside of the VisibilityChangedSignal or InheritedVisibilityChangedSignal signal. "INVALID" don't mean the empty handle. It might return the valid handle. But it doesn't mean the visibility changed actor. if this API called outside of VisibilityChangedSignal or InheritedVisibilityChangedSignal signal.
members.returns: The actor who trigger the visibility changed signal.

members.name: ChildAddedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when a child is added to this actor.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidMyCallbackName(Actorchild); child: The child that has been added.
notes: Use this signal with caution. Changing the parent of the actor within this callback is possible, but DALi will prevent further signals being sent.
members.returns: The signal to connect to

members.name: ChildRemovedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when a child is removed from this actor.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidMyCallbackName(Actorchild); child: The child that has been removed.
notes: Use this signal with caution. Changing the parent of the actor within this callback is possible, but DALi will prevent further signals being sent.
If the child actor is moved from one actor to another, then this signal will be emitted followed immediately by an ChildAddedSignal() on the new parent.
members.returns: The signal to connect to

members.name: ChildOrderChangedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when an actor's children change their sibling order.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidMyCallbackName(Actorchild); child The child actor that changed order
members.returns: The signal to connect to

members.name: InterceptTouchedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when intercepting the actor's touch event.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidMyCallbackName(Actoractor); actor The actor to intercept If you connect interceptTouchSignal to parentActor. Dali::DevelActor::InterceptTouchedSignal(parent).Connect(&application, interceptFunctor); When interceptFunctor returns false, the touch event callbacks are called in the same order childFunctor -> parentFunctor. If interceptFunctor returns true, it means that the TouchEvent was intercepted. So the child actor will not be able to receive touch events. Only the parentFunctor is called.
notes: TouchEvent callbacks are called from the last child in the order of the parent's actor. The InterceptTouchEvent callback is to intercept the touch event in the parent. So, if the parent interepts the touch event, the child cannot receive the touch event.

example Actor parent = Actor::New(); Actor child = Actor::New(); parent.Add(child); child.TouchedSignal().Connect(&application, childFunctor); parent.TouchedSignal().Connect(&application, parentFunctor); The touch event callbacks are called in the order childFunctor -> parentFunctor.
members.returns: The signal to connect to

members.name: InterceptWheelSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when intercepting the actor's wheel event.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidMyCallbackName(Actoractor); actor The actor to intercept If you connect InterceptWheelSignal to parentActor. Dali::DevelActor::InterceptWheelSignal(parent).Connect(&application, interceptFunctor); When interceptFunctor returns false, the wheel event callbacks are called in the same order childFunctor -> parentFunctor. If interceptFunctor returns true, it means that the WheelEvent was intercepted. So the child actor will not be able to receive wheel events. Only the parentFunctor is called.
notes: WheelEvent callbacks are called from the last child in the order of the parent's actor. The InterceptWheelEvent callback is to intercept the wheel event in the parent. So, if the parent interepts the wheel event, the child cannot receive the Wheel event.

example Actor parent = Actor::New(); Actor child = Actor::New(); parent.Add(child); child.WheelEventSignal().Connect(&application, childFunctor); parent.WheelEventSignal().Connect(&application, parentFunctor); The wheel event callbacks are called in the order childFunctor -> parentFunctor.
members.returns: The signal to connect to

members.name: SetNeedGesturePropagation
members.brief: This is used when the parent actor wants to listen to gesture events.
notes: example The child is overlapped on the parent. Currently, if you tap a child, the parent cannot listen to the tap event. Now, If set to SetNeedGesturePropagation(true), the parent can receive gesture events. Please call this setting inside a gesture callback, it will be reset after the gesture callback is called. { Actorparent=Actor::New(); Actorchild=Actor::New(); parent.Add(child); parentTapDetector=TapGestureDetector::New(); childTapDetector=TapGestureDetector::New(); parentTapDetector.Attach(parent); childTapDetector.Attach(child); parentTapDetector.DetectedSignal().Connect(this,&OnParentTap); childTapDetector.DetectedSignal().Connect(this,&OnChildTap); } voidOnChildTap(Dali::Actoractor,constDali::TapGesture&tap) { //IfyousetSetNeedGesturePropagationtotruehere,theparentactorcanalsolistentoevents Dali::DevelActor::SetNeedGesturePropagation(Self(),true); }

members.name: SwitchParent
members.detailed: Switch parent in the same tree. This method changes the actor's parent with keeping on scene state. Both of current parent Actor and new parent Actor must already be added on Scene. This method don't emit notification about add/remove and on/off scene.

params.name: newParent
params.description: An actor to be a new parent of this actor.

members.name: HitTestResultSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when an actor is hit through hit-test.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidMyCallbackName(Actoractor); actor The actor to intercept If you connect HitTestResultSignal to topActor. Dali::DevelActor::HitTestResultSignal(topActor).Connect(&application, hitTestResultFunctor); If the hitTestResult Functor returns false, it passes the hit-test and starts the hit-test again from the next lower actor. So the bottomActor can be hit and receive touch events. If hitTestResult returns true, it means that it has been hit. So it receives a TouchEvent from itself.
notes: This callback is called when the actor is hit. If true is returned, TouchEvent is called from the this actor. If false is returned, the hit test starts again from the next lower actor.

example Actor topActor = Actor::New(); Actor bottomActor = Actor::New(); topActor.TouchedSignal().Connect(&application, topActorFunctor); bottomActor.TouchedSignal().Connect(&application, bottomActorFunctor); The two actors have no relationship. So when the topActor is touched, the event cannot be propagated to the bottomActor.
warnings: You MUST NOT change the scene tree or RenderTask during this signal.
members.returns: The signal to connect to

members.name: GetWorldTransform
members.detailed: Get the world transform of the actor. This calculates the world transform from scratch using only event side properties - it does not rely on the update thread to have already calculated the transform.
members.returns: The world transform matrix

params.name: actor
params.description: The actor for which to calculate the world transform

members.name: GetWorldColor
members.detailed: Get the world color of the actor. This calcualtes the world color of the actor from scratch using only event side properties. It does not rely on the update thread to have already calculated the color.
members.returns: the world color

params.name: actor
params.description: The actor to calculate the world color for

members.name: IsEffectivelyVisible
members.detailed: Get the effective visibility of the actor. This calculates the effective visibility of the actor from scratch using only event side properties. It does not rely on the update thread to have already calculated the visibility. The effective visibility takes into account the actor's own visibility property as well as the visibility of all its parents in the hierarchy.
members.returns: False if the actor or any of its parents is not visible, or if the scene is off. True otherwise.

params.name: actor
params.description: The actor to calculate the effective visibility for

members.name: LookAt
members.detailed: Rotate the actor look at specific position. It will change the actor's orientation property. This calculates the world transform from scratch using only event side properties - it does not rely on the update thread to have already calculated the transform.
notes: Target position should be setup by world coordinates.
The result of invalid input is not determined. (ex : forward vector or actor-to-target vector has same direction with up, One of them is ZERO)
members.signature: void LookAt(Actor actor, Vector3 target, Vector3 up, Vector3 localForward, Vector3 localUp)

params.name: actor
params.description: The actor for which to calculate the look at orientation.

params.name: target
params.description: The target world position to look at.

params.name: up
params.description: The up vector after target look at. Default is +Y axis.

params.name: localForward
params.description: The forward vector of actor when it's orientation is not applied. Default is +Z axis.

params.name: localUp
params.description: The up vector of actor when it's orientation is not applied. Default is +Y axis.

members.name: IsHittable
members.detailed: Query whether the actor is actually hittable. This method checks whether the actor is sensitive, has the visibility flag set to true and is not fully transparent.
members.returns: true, if it can be hit, false otherwise.

params.name: actor
params.description: The actor for whether is hittable or not

members.name: Type
members.brief: Enumeration for texture filtering mode.

enumvalues.name: NONE
enumvalues.brief: Use GL defaults (minification NEAREST_MIPMAP_LINEAR, magnification LINEAR).

enumvalues.name: DEFAULT
enumvalues.brief: Use Dali defaults (minification LINEAR, magnification LINEAR).

enumvalues.name: NEAREST
enumvalues.brief: Filter nearest.

enumvalues.name: LINEAR
enumvalues.brief: Filter linear.

enumvalues.name: NEAREST_MIPMAP_NEAREST
enumvalues.brief: Chooses the mipmap that most closely matches the size of the pixel being textured and uses the GL_NEAREST criterion (the texture element closest to the specified texture coordinates) to produce a texture value.

enumvalues.name: LINEAR_MIPMAP_NEAREST
enumvalues.brief: Chooses the mipmap that most closely matches the size of the pixel being textured and uses the GL_LINEAR criterion (a weighted average of the four texture elements that are closest to the specified texture coordinates) to produce a texture value.

enumvalues.name: NEAREST_MIPMAP_LINEAR
enumvalues.brief: Chooses the two mipmaps that most closely match the size of the pixel being textured and uses the GL_NEAREST criterion (the texture element closest to the specified texture coordinates ) to produce a texture value from each mipmap. The final texture value is a weighted average of those two values.

enumvalues.name: LINEAR_MIPMAP_LINEAR
enumvalues.brief: Chooses the two mipmaps that most closely match the size of the pixel being textured and uses the GL_LINEAR criterion (a weighted average of the texture elements that are closest to the specified texture coordinates) to produce a texture value from each mipmap. The final texture value is a weighted average of those two values.

compounds.name: Dali::HitTestAlgorithm
compounds.brief: This namespace is provided for application developers to do hit-test for the actors.
compounds.detailed: Hit Test Algorithm: Hit testing is dependent on the camera used, which is specific to each RenderTask. For each RenderTask, hit testing starts from the top-most layer and we go through all the layers until we have a hit or there are none left. Before we perform a hit test within a layer, we check if all the layer's parents meet the conditions defined by the function ((e.g. whether it is visible)). If they are not, we skip hit testing the actors in that layer altogether. Otherwise, we walk through the actor tree within a layer to check whether the actors within the actor tree should be hit-tested. The following pseudocode gives an example of what the function can typically check, which should normally be provided by the application code: HIT-TEST-FUNCTION(ACTOR,TRAVERSE-TYPE) { if(TRAVERSE-TYPE==CHECK_ACTOR)//Checkwhethercurrentactorshouldbehit-tested { if(ACTOR-IS-VISIBLE&& ACTOR-WORLD-COLOR-IS-NOT-TRANSPARENT) { HITTABLE=TRUE } } elseif(TRAVERSE-TYPE==DESCEND_ACTOR_TREE) { if(ACTOR-IS-VISIBLE) { HITTABLE=TRUE } } } The following pseudocode explains how the algorithm performs the hit-test with the above functor: HIT-TEST-WITHIN-LAYER(ACTOR) { //Depth-firsttraversalwithincurrentlayer,visitingparentfirst //Checkwhethercurrentactorshouldbehit-tested IF(HIT-TEST-FUNCTION(ACTOR,CHECK_ACTOR)&& ACTOR-HAS-NON-ZERO-SIZE) { //Hit-testcurrentactor IF(ACTOR-HIT) { IF(DISTANCE-TO-ACTOR<DISTANCE-TO-LAST-HIT-ACTOR) { //Thecurrentactoristheclosestactorthatwasunderneaththetouch LAST-HIT-ACTOR=CURRENT-ACTOR } } } //Keepcheckingchildren,incasewehitsomethingcloser FOR-EACHCHILD(inorder) { IF(HIT-TEST-FUNCTION(ACTOR,DESCEND_ACTOR_TREE)&& ACTOR-IS-NOT-A-LAYER) { //Continuetraversalforthischild'ssub-tree HIT-TEST-WITHIN-LAYER(CHILD) } //elseweskipthesub-treewithfromthischild } }

members.name: HitTestFunction
members.brief: Definition of a hit-test function to use in HitTest() method to check if the actor is hittable (e.g. touchable or focusable).

members.name: HitTest
members.brief: Given screen coordinates, this method returns the hit actor & the local coordinates relative to the top-left (0.0f, 0.0f, 0.5f) of the actor.
members.detailed: An actor is only hittable if the actor meets all the conditions defined by the given function (see HitTestAlgorithm). Typically, if an actor has a zero size or its world color is fully transparent, it should not be hittable; and if an actor's visibility flag is unset, its children should not be hittable either.
members.signature: bool HitTest(Stage stage, const Vector2 &screenCoordinates, Results &results, HitTestFunction func, const Integration::Scene::TouchPropagationType propagationType=Integration::Scene::TouchPropagationType::PARENT)
members.returns: true if something was hit

params.description: The results of the hit-test, only modified if something is hit

params.name: func
params.description: The function to use in the hit-test algorithm.

params.name: propagationType
params.description: If Integration::Scene::TouchPropagationType::GEOMETRY, hittest works in a geometry way.

members.name: SetPanGesturePredictionMode
members.brief: Called by adaptor to set the pan gesture prediction mode from an environment variable.

members.name: SetPanGesturePredictionAmount
members.brief: Called by adaptor to set the prediction amount of the pan gesture from an environment variable.

members.name: SetPanGestureMaximumPredictionAmount
members.brief: Sets the upper bound of the prediction amount for clamping from an environment variable.

members.name: SetPanGestureMinimumPredictionAmount
members.brief: Sets the lower bound of the prediction amount for clamping from an environment variable.

members.name: SetPanGesturePredictionAmountAdjustment
members.brief: Sets the prediction amount to adjust when the pan velocity is changed from an environment variable. If the pan velocity is accelerating, the prediction amount will be increased by the specified amount until it reaches the upper bound. If the pan velocity is decelerating, the prediction amount will be decreased by the specified amount until it reaches the lower bound.

members.name: SetPanGestureSmoothingMode
members.brief: Called to set how pan gestures smooth input.

members.name: SetPanGestureSmoothingAmount
members.brief: Sets the smoothing amount of the pan gesture.

params.name: amount
params.description: The smoothing amount [0.0f,1.0f] - 0.0f would be no smoothing, 1.0f maximum smoothing

members.name: SetPanGestureUseActualTimes
members.brief: Sets whether to use actual times of the real gesture and frames or not.

params.name: value
params.description: True = use actual times, False = use perfect values

members.name: SetPanGestureInterpolationTimeRange
members.brief: Sets the interpolation time range (ms) of past points to use (with weights) when interpolating.

members.name: SetPanGestureScalarOnlyPredictionEnabled
members.brief: Sets whether to use scalar only prediction, which when enabled, ignores acceleration.

members.name: SetPanGestureTwoPointPredictionEnabled
members.brief: Sets whether to use two point prediction. This combines two interpolated points to get more steady acceleration and velocity values.

members.name: SetPanGestureTwoPointInterpolatePastTime
members.brief: Sets the time in the past to interpolate the second point when using two point interpolation.

members.name: SetPanGestureTwoPointVelocityBias
members.brief: Sets the two point velocity bias. This is the ratio of first and second points to use for velocity.

members.name: SetPanGestureTwoPointAccelerationBias
members.brief: Sets the two point acceleration bias. This is the ratio of first and second points to use for acceleration.

members.name: SetPanGestureMultitapSmoothingRange
members.brief: Sets the range of time (ms) of points in the history to perform multitap smoothing with (if enabled).

members.name: SetPanGestureMinimumDistance
members.brief: Sets the minimum distance required to start a pan gesture.

members.name: SetPanGestureMinimumPanEvents
members.brief: Sets the minimum number of touch events to start a pan.

members.name: SetPinchGestureMinimumDistance
members.brief: Sets the minimum distance required to start a pinch gesture.

members.name: SetPinchGestureMinimumTouchEvents
members.brief: Sets the minimum touch events required before a pinch can be started.

members.name: SetPinchGestureMinimumTouchEventsAfterStart
members.brief: Sets the minimum touch events required after a pinch started.

members.name: SetRotationGestureMinimumTouchEvents
members.brief: Sets the minimum touch events required before a rotation can be started.

members.name: SetRotationGestureMinimumTouchEventsAfterStart
members.brief: Sets the minimum touch events required after a rotation started.

members.name: SetLongPressMinimumHoldingTime
members.brief: Sets the minimum holding time required to be recognized as a long press gesture.

members.name: SetTapMaximumAllowedTime
members.brief: Sets the maximum allowed time required to be recognized as a multi tap gesture (millisecond)
members.detailed: This is the maximum allowable time interval to recognize as multi-tap. If taps come in within this time, they are recognized as multi-tap.
notes: If it's a double tap, it's like this: |(touch down <recognizerTime--> touch up) < wihtin maximumAllowedTime --> (touch down <recognizerTime--> touch up)|

members.name: SetTapRecognizerTime
members.brief: Sets the recognizer time required to be recognized as a tap gesture (millisecond)
members.detailed: This time is from touch down to touch up to recognize the tap gesture.
notes: The tab is like below: touch down <recognizerTime--> touch up If the time between touch down and touch up is longer than recognizer time, it is not recognized as a tap gesture.

members.name: SetTapMaximumMotionAllowedDistance
members.brief: Sets the recognizer distance required to be recognized as a tap gesture.
members.detailed: This distance is from touch down to touch up to recognize the tap gesture.
notes: The tab is like below: touch down <distance--> touch up If the distance between touch down and touch up is longer than distance, it is not recognized as a tap gesture. Default value is 20

members.name: FIRST_VALID_PIXEL_FORMAT
members.brief: For asserting that a variable has a valid pixel format.
members.detailed: Sync it to the first value above.

members.name: LAST_VALID_PIXEL_FORMAT
members.brief: For asserting that a variable has a valid pixel format.
members.detailed: Sync it to the last value above.

members.name: IsCompressed
members.brief: Whether specified pixel format is compressed or not.
members.returns: True if pixel format is compressed type. False otherwise.

members.name: HasAlpha
members.brief: Whether specified pixel format contains an alpha value.
members.returns: true if format has alpha, false otherwise

members.name: GetBytesPerPixel
members.brief: Returns The number of bytes per pixel for the specified pixel format.
members.returns: The number of bytes per pixel

members.name: GetAlphaOffsetAndMask
members.brief: Returns the offset of the byte containing the alpha value from the start of the pixel data and the bitmask of that byte to get the alpha value.
members.detailed: For example, in case of Pixel::RGBA4444, byteOffset value is 1 and bitMask value is 0x0f. It means the second byte contains the alpha value and the last 4 bits of the byte is the alpha value. Bitmask is zero if the pixelFormat does not support alpha.
members.signature: DALI_CORE_API void GetAlphaOffsetAndMask(Format pixelFormat, int &byteOffset, int &bitMask)

params.name: byteOffset
params.description: The byte offset of the byte containing the alpha value

params.name: bitMask
params.description: The bitmask of the byte to get the alpha value

members.name: Type
members.brief: Enumeration for the controls of how this renderer uses its stencil properties and writes to the color buffer.

enumvalues.name: NONE
enumvalues.brief: Do not write to either color or stencil buffer (But will potentially render to depth buffer).

enumvalues.name: AUTO
enumvalues.brief: Managed by the Actor Clipping API. This is the default.

enumvalues.name: COLOR
enumvalues.brief: Ingore stencil properties. Write to the color buffer.

enumvalues.name: STENCIL
enumvalues.brief: Use the stencil properties. Do not write to the color buffer.

enumvalues.name: COLOR_STENCIL
enumvalues.brief: Use the stencil properties AND Write to the color buffer.

compounds.name: Dali::RenderMode::Type
compounds.brief: Enumeration for the controls of how this renderer uses its stencil properties and writes to the color buffer.

members.name: NONE
members.brief: Do not write to either color or stencil buffer (But will potentially render to depth buffer).

members.name: AUTO
members.brief: Managed by the Actor Clipping API. This is the default.

members.name: COLOR
members.brief: Ingore stencil properties. Write to the color buffer.

members.name: STENCIL
members.brief: Use the stencil properties. Do not write to the color buffer.

members.name: COLOR_STENCIL
members.brief: Use the stencil properties AND Write to the color buffer.

compounds.name: Dali::ResizePolicy
compounds.brief: Enumeration for size negotiation resize policies.

members.name: Type
members.brief: Enumeration for ResizePolicy types.

enumvalues.name: FIXED
enumvalues.brief: Size is fixed as set by SetSize

enumvalues.name: USE_NATURAL_SIZE
enumvalues.brief: Size is to use the actor's natural size

enumvalues.name: FILL_TO_PARENT
enumvalues.brief: Size is to fill up to the actor's parent's bounds. Aspect ratio is not maintained.

enumvalues.name: SIZE_RELATIVE_TO_PARENT
enumvalues.brief: The actor's size will be ( ParentSize * SizeModeFactor ).

enumvalues.name: SIZE_FIXED_OFFSET_FROM_PARENT
enumvalues.brief: The actor's size will be ( ParentSize + SizeModeFactor ).

enumvalues.name: FIT_TO_CHILDREN
enumvalues.brief: Size will adjust to wrap around all children

enumvalues.name: DIMENSION_DEPENDENCY
enumvalues.brief: One dimension is dependent on the other

enumvalues.name: USE_ASSIGNED_SIZE
enumvalues.brief: The size will be assigned to the actor

members.name: DEFAULT
members.brief: Default resize policy.

members.name: EnumStringToInteger

params.description: A pointer to an array with the enumeration to string equivalents.

params.name: tableCount
params.description: Number of items in the array.

members.name: GetEnumeration
members.brief: Chooses the appropriate enumeration for the provided string from the given table.
members.signature: bool GetEnumeration(const char *value, const StringEnum *table, uint32_t tableCount, T &result)
members.returns: True if the value was found from the table

params.name: value
params.description: The string equivalent (case-insensitive, comma separate to OR values).

params.name: table
params.description: A pointer to an array with the enumeration to string equivalents.

params.name: tableCount
params.description: Number of items in the array.

members.name: GetEnumerationProperty
members.brief: Gets the enumeration value from an enumeration property. An enumeration property is a property that can be set with either an INTEGER or STRING.
members.signature: bool GetEnumerationProperty(const Property::Value &propertyValue, const StringEnum *table, uint32_t tableCount, T &result)
members.returns: True if the value was found successfully AND the value has changed. This is to allow the caller to do nothing if there is no change.

params.name: propertyValue
params.description: The property containing the int or string value.

params.name: table
params.description: A pointer to an array with the enumeration to string equivalents.

params.name: tableCount
params.description: Number of items in the array.

params.name: result
params.description: The enum value. This is not modified if the enumeration could not be converted.

members.name: GetBitmaskEnumerationProperty
members.brief: Gets the enumeration value from a bitmask enumeration property. An enumeration property is a property that can be set with either an INTEGER, STRING or an ARRAY of STRING.
members.signature: bool GetBitmaskEnumerationProperty(const Property::Value &propertyValue, const Scripting::StringEnum *table, uint32_t tableCount, T &result)
members.returns: True if the value was found successfully AND the value has changed. This is to allow the caller to do nothing if there is no change.

params.name: propertyValue
params.description: The property containing the int, string or and array of string values.

params.name: table
params.description: A pointer to an array with the enumeration to string equivalents.

params.name: tableCount
params.description: Number of items in the array.

params.name: result
params.description: The enum value. This is not modified if the enumeration could not be converted.

members.name: GetEnumerationName
members.brief: Chooses the appropriate string for the provided enumeration from the given table.

members.returns: The equivalent enumeration for the given string. Will return NULL if the value does not exist

params.name: table
params.description: A pointer to an array with the enumeration to string equivalents.

params.name: tableCount
params.description: Number of items in the array.

members.name: Type
members.brief: Enumeration for specifying the action to take when the stencil (or depth) test fails during stencil test.

enumvalues.name: ZERO
enumvalues.brief: Sets the stencil buffer value to 0

enumvalues.name: KEEP
enumvalues.brief: Keeps the current value

enumvalues.name: REPLACE
enumvalues.brief: Sets the stencil buffer value to ref, as specified by glStencilFunc

enumvalues.name: INCREMENT
enumvalues.brief: Increments the current stencil buffer value. Clamps to the maximum representable unsigned value

enumvalues.name: DECREMENT
enumvalues.brief: Decrements the current stencil buffer value. Clamps to 0

enumvalues.name: INVERT
enumvalues.brief: Bitwise inverts the current stencil buffer value

enumvalues.name: INCREMENT_WRAP
enumvalues.brief: Increments the current stencil buffer value. Wraps stencil buffer value to zero when incrementing the maximum representable unsigned value

enumvalues.name: DECREMENT_WRAP
enumvalues.brief: Decrements the current stencil buffer value. Wraps stencil buffer value to the maximum representable unsigned value when decrementing a stencil buffer value of zero

members.name: DALI_DEVEL_PROPERTY_REGISTRATION_INTERNAL
members.brief: These macros are used internally by the property macros. Use the the property macros in the section below this one (without the _INTERNAL postfix) when defining properties. These internal macros exist as to perform the compile-time check on the enumeration order, the COUNTER macro is used twice. Using it twice within the same macro would result in two different values.

members.name: DALI_DEVEL_PROPERTY_REGISTRATION
members.brief: These macros are used to define properties for implementations of CustomActor.
members.detailed: These macros should be used when defining devel properties They provide the following benefits: A standard and consistent way to define properties. Concise definition promotes readability, especially with large numbers of properties. Provides a built-in compile-time check. This checks the order of the properties within the enumeration match the order of the property macros. Note: This check is not performed for animatable properties. Enforces how properties are enumerated in the object handles header file. Note: The compile-type check will produce the following message on failure: error: invalid application of 'sizeof' to incomplete type 'Dali::CompileTimeAssertBool<false>' Macro usage example: Within the your object's implementation cpp: #include<dali/public-api/object/devel-property-helper.h> ... DALI_TYPE_REGISTRATION_BEGIN(MyApp::MyCustomActor,Dali::CustomActor,Create) DALI_DEVEL_PROPERTY_REGISTRATION(MyApp,MyCustomActor,"myProperty",INTEGER,MY_DEVEL_PROPERTY) DALI_TYPE_REGISTRATION_END() Within your handle's header: #include<dali/public-api/common/dali-common.h> ///<@briefThestartandendpropertyrangesforthiscontrol. enumPropertyRange { PROPERTY_START_INDEX=Dali::PROPERTY_REGISTRATION_START_INDEX, PROPERTY_END_INDEX=PROPERTY_START_INDEX+1000 }; ///<@briefAnenumerationofpropertiesbelongingtotheButtonclass. structProperty { enum { MY_PROPERTY=PROPERTY_START_INDEX///<@briefname"myProperty",typeInteger }; }; Using these macros have certain prerequisites on how the property enumeration is defined. Please see the Programming Guide (within the generated Doxygen) for full details.
